calculated_metrics.confidence_note: Cost per outcome calculation is not available. This organisation conducts significant multi-domain work (e.g., human education, environmental conservation). Dividing the total budget solely by quantified animal outcomes would artificially inflate the cost and misrepresent their financial efficiency.

details.formula: Calculation aborted due to multi-domain operations.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

calculated_metrics.confidence_note: IES is a Back-of-the-Envelope Calculation (BOTEC) to standardize impact across interventions and species. It relies on philosophical and empirical assumptions.

calculated_metrics.name: Impact Equivalency Score (IES)

details.criteria: Pass: <= 2 years of expenditure. | Warn: 2-5 years. | Fail: > 5 years.
Pass: >= 6 months of operating expenses. | Warn: 3-6 months. | Fail: < 3 months.
Pass: At least one intervention is classified as Tier 1 (Systemic Change) or Tier 2 (Prevention & Scalable Care). | Warn: All verifiable interventions are Tier 3 (Direct Care & Indirect Action).

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.calculation: No valid counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.criteria: Pass: At least one metric has a quantified counterfactual baseline. | Warn: A qualitative baseline is provided, but lacks quantified data. | Fail: No valid counterfactual baseline is provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline with a source quote.

details.calculation: Financials with income breakdown are missing.
details.criteria: Pass: Government grants are < 80% of total income. | Fail: Government grants are >= 80% of total income.

details.calculation: Mixed portfolio with minority focus on high-neglectedness areas (Wild Animals: 49%, Companion Animals: 51%).
details.criteria: Pass: >= 50% of beneficiaries are high-neglectedness (farmed/wild). | Warn: 1-49% are high-neglectedness. | Fail: 0% are high-neglectedness.
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts.
details.criteria: Bonus: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'Tragically though, our beautiful girls, Ha Long, Thang Long and Tulip, and our lovely boy, Stephen, lived only a short while in our care, their bodies simply too damaged to repair.'
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.
details.criteria: Bonus: Euthanasia/live-release data is disclosed. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found. | N/A: Not applicable for non-sheltering organisations.
details.elaboration: This check rewards transparency for a sensitive operational metric.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

source.quote: Administration and governance 9%
Income generation 25%
Moon Bear Protection 55%: Over half our program spend was directed to our flagship program which saw some impressive results in 2024 - our 700th bear rescued and our physiotherapy work expanded.

Animal Sentience and Welfare 9%: Our wide-ranging work to enrich the lives of captive animals and free those that we can focused mainly on ending elephant riding and providing help and advice to government rescue centres.

Cat and Dog Welfare 9%: Our work to improve the welfare of cats and dogs continued, with highlights including the expansion of our long-running China cat and dog shelter program to shelters in Vietnam.

Public Education 27%: Supporting our three main programs is our critical community education work, which is changing hearts and minds throughout Asia. We target schools and communities where bear bile farming is common.
We spent $10.5m directly on our programs in 2024.
RUNNING COSTS $15.94m
GLOBAL INCOME $20.68M (all amounts in US$)
Rescued 13 precious moon bears and brought them home to our sanctuaries in Vietnam.
Provided world-class care for the 297 bears living peacefully in our sanctuaries, 99 in China and 198 in Vietnam.
Performed 221 full-body health checks on our rescued bears, including 23 major surgeries.
Helped 13,350 shelter dogs and cats in China.
Helped neuter 467 cats.
Gave 15 shelter dogs and cats new wheelchairs.
Helped rescue and release over 23,000 songbirds.
Helped care for 14 rescued elephants.
Rescued 2 more elephants from elephant-riding tourism. Kham On and Y'Mam are now free to roam for the rest of their days.
Provided food for 25 macaques we previously helped release.
Donated 10,400 doses of rabies vaccine to dogs in Vietnamese shelters.
In 2024, our three smallies clinics treated 436 companion animals - 264 dogs and 172 cats.
In 2024, our three smallies clinics treated 436 companion animals - 264 dogs and 172 cats.
Helped release 31 macaques into protected areas.
Our Indonesian partner, Scorpion Foundation, rescued, rehabilitated and released 8 former pet macaques into Barumun Wildlife Reserve.
Our Indonesian partner, Jakarta Animal Aid Network, released 12 macaques onto a protected island following their rescue from the pet trade and rehabilitation at their rescue centre in Bali.
You made 2024 an amazing year for Asia's animals. Because of your loyal and generous support, we were able to help countless animals in need.

source.quote: We delivered talks on the social, emotional and cognitive lives of pigs and understanding animal welfare to 50 children in Chengdu primary schools.

metrics.context_qualifier: into protected areas in Indonesia

source.quote: Helped release 31 macaques into protected areas.

metrics.context_qualifier: in China since 2018

source.quote: Our 2024 survey shows that 23 Chinese zoos have stopped the use of animals in performances since our last survey in 2018.

metrics.context_qualifier: through Vietnamese NGO Forever Wheelchair
metrics.metric_name: Dogs and cats provided with wheelchairs

source.quote: Provided 15 dogs and cats with new wheelchairs through Vietnamese NGO Forever Wheelchair.

metrics.context_qualifier: including a spotted dove and a little owl

source.quote: 15 birds were rescued, including a spotted dove and a little owl.

metrics.context_qualifier: in Vietnam, out of 68 captive elephants

source.quote: Cared for 14 elephants of the 68 believed to be in captivity. Most of them were rescued from carrying tourists.

metrics.context_qualifier: in Vietnam

source.quote: Rescued 13 precious moon bears and brought them home to our sanctuaries in Vietnam.

metrics.context_qualifier: by Jakarta Animal Aid Network onto a protected island, Indonesia

source.quote: Our Indonesian partner, Jakarta Animal Aid Network, released 12 macaques onto a protected island following their rescue from the pet trade and rehabilitation at their rescue centre in Bali.

metrics.context_qualifier: by reporting to Chinese authorities
metrics.metric_name: Restaurants stopped from selling dog/cat meat

source.quote: Stopped 10 restaurants from selling dog and/or cat meat by reporting them to the Chinese authorities.

metrics.context_qualifier: at China sanctuary, bringing total to 25

source.quote: We installed 10 more at our China sanctuary in 2024, bringing the total number of tunnels there to 25.

metrics.context_qualifier: by Scorpion Foundation into Barumun Wildlife Reserve, Indonesia

source.quote: Our Indonesian partner, Scorpion Foundation, rescued, rehabilitated and released 8 former pet macaques into Barumun Wildlife Reserve.

metrics.context_qualifier: in Vietnam, bringing total to 35

source.quote: Planted 3 new community herbal gardens, bringing the total number across Vietnam to 35.

metrics.context_qualifier: from elephant-riding tourism in Vietnam

source.quote: Rescued 2 more elephants from elephant-riding tourism. Kham On and Y'Mam are now free to roam for the rest of their days.
Tragically though, our beautiful girls, Ha Long, Thang Long and Tulip, and our lovely boy, Stephen, lived only a short while in our care, their bodies simply too damaged to repair.
